name: Optimize nix-store caching for Rust builds in unit2nix

description: Baseline noop rebuild benchmark attempt using hyperfine failed because hyperfine is not available in the environment.
Second baseline attempt failed because the shell pipeline fed no timing samples into Python; need a more robust timing harness before comparing caching changes.

Baseline noop rebuild benchmark established with a simple Python timing harness around `nix build .#sample --no-link`; first two runs were coldish, last three were warm, so this is a noisy but usable starting point.
Refined baseline by discarding first three coldish runs and averaging only steady-state noop rebuild timings; warm-cache baseline for `nix build .#sample --no-link` is about 182 ms.

Added two low-risk cache improvements: local source filtering now ignores common result/.direnv noise and git sources use fixed-output `pkgs.fetchgit` when sha256 is available, cutting warm noop rebuild time substantially without changing benchmark semantics.
